through.en: from the beginning all the way to the end

ex.en: She read the letter through before replying.
ex.ko: 그녀는 답장하기 전에 편지를 끝까지 읽었다.

through.en: in one side and out of the other side of something

ex.en: The train went through the tunnel.
Are you through with the dishes?

throw.en: send something through the air using your hand and arm

ex.en: He threw the ball to his sister.
That was a great throw!

thursday.en: the day of the week after Wednesday and before Friday

ex.en: We have a meeting on Thursday.

ticket.en: a piece of paper showing that you have paid to travel or enter somewhere

ex.en: She bought a ticket for the concert.

ticket.en: an official notice saying you must pay money for breaking a traffic rule

ex.en: He got a parking ticket.

tiger.en: a large wild cat with orange fur and black stripes

ex.en: Tigers live mostly in Asia.
Hold on tight!
These shoes are too tight.

tight.en: having very little extra time or money

ex.en: We have a tight schedule this week.
He timed the race with a stopwatch.

time.en: the thing we measure in hours, minutes, and seconds

ex.en: I don't have much time today.
This is the third time I've called you.
ex.ko: 이번이 너한테 세 번째로 전화하는 거야.

tired.en: feeling like you need to rest or sleep

ex.en: I'm so tired after work.

tired.en: feeling bored or annoyed because something has happened too many times

ex.en: I'm tired of hearing the same excuse.

to.en: used to show the direction or place something is going toward

ex.en: She went to the store.

to.en: used before a verb to show purpose or as part of the infinitive

ex.en: I want to learn English.

today.en: this day, the day that is happening now

ex.en: What day is today?
I have a lot of work today.
We had dinner together.

toilet.en: a room with a toilet where you can wash and use the bathroom

ex.en: Where is the toilet?

toilet.en: the bowl-shaped fixture you sit on to use the bathroom

ex.en: He flushed the toilet.

tomato.en: a soft red fruit that is eaten as a vegetable

ex.en: She sliced a tomato for the salad.
Tomorrow is Friday.
See you tomorrow.

tonight.en: during the evening or night of this day

ex.en: Let's watch a movie tonight.

tonight.en: the evening or night of this day

ex.en: Tonight will be cold.

too.en: more than is good, needed, or wanted

ex.en: This coffee is too hot.
I like pizza too.

tool.en: an object used to do a particular job

ex.en: A hammer is a useful tool.

tooth.en: one of the hard white parts in your mouth used for biting and chewing

ex.en: Brush your teeth twice a day.
We reached the top of the mountain.

top.en: being the best or highest in rank

ex.en: She is a top student in her class.

topic.en: the subject being discussed or written about

ex.en: Let's change the topic.

touch.en: put your hand or fingers on something

ex.en: Please don't touch the paintings.

touch.en: come into physical contact with something

ex.en: The two wires touched and made a spark.
Let's keep in touch.

towel.en: a piece of cloth used for drying your body

ex.en: She dried her hair with a towel.

tower.en: a tall, narrow building or structure

ex.en: They visited the famous tower.

town.en: a place with houses and shops that is smaller than a city

ex.en: He grew up in a small town.

toy.en: an object that children play with

ex.en: The baby is playing with a toy.

train.en: teach a person or animal a skill through regular practice

ex.en: She trains young athletes every day.

train.en: a line of connected cars that runs on rails, used for travel

ex.en: We took the train to Busan.

train.en: practice regularly to prepare for something

ex.en: He is training for the marathon.

travel.en: the activity of going from one place to another, especially a long way

ex.en: Travel is one of her favorite hobbies.
ex.ko: 여행은 그녀가 가장 좋아하는 취미 중 하나다.

travel.en: go from one place to another, especially a long distance

ex.en: They traveled across Europe last summer.

tree.en: a tall plant with a trunk, branches, and leaves

ex.en: There is a big tree in the yard.

trip.en: catch your foot on something and almost fall

ex.en: She tripped over a rock.
ex.ko: 그녀는 돌에 발이 걸려 넘어질 뻔했다.

trip.en: a journey to a place and back

ex.en: We're planning a trip to Jeju.

trousers.en: a piece of clothing that covers your legs separately, from the waist down

ex.en: He bought a new pair of trousers.
ex.ko: 그는 새 바지를 한 벌 샀다.

truck.en: a large vehicle used for carrying goods

ex.en: The truck delivered furniture to the house.

true.en: in agreement with fact or reality

ex.en: Is it true that you're moving?

true.en: real and genuine, not fake or pretended

ex.en: They have a true friendship.

try.en: make an effort to do something

ex.en: I'll try to finish it by tomorrow.

try.en: test or sample something to see if you like it

ex.en: Would you like to try this cake?
It's worth a try.

tube.en: a long, hollow object shaped like a pipe

ex.en: He squeezed the toothpaste tube.

tuesday.en: the day of the week after Monday and before Wednesday

ex.en: The test is on Tuesday.

turkey.en: a large bird often eaten at Thanksgiving or Christmas

ex.en: They cooked a turkey for dinner.

tv.en: an electronic device you watch programs and movies on

ex.en: They watched TV all evening.
There are twelve months in a year.
She is twenty years old.
I have two brothers.

type.en: write something using a computer or phone keyboard

ex.en: She typed a message on her phone.

type.en: a group of things or people with similar features

ex.en: What type of music do you like?

ugly.en: not attractive or pleasant to look at

ex.en: That's such an ugly building.

umbrella.cue: 비 올 때 펴서 쓰는 물건
umbrella.en: a device you hold over your head to stay dry in the rain

ex.en: Don't forget your umbrella; it's raining.
ex.ko: 우산 잊지 마, 비 오고 있어.

uncle.en: the brother of your father or mother, or the husband of your aunt

ex.en: My uncle lives in Seoul.

under.en: in or to a lower position than something

ex.en: The cat is sleeping under the table.

under.en: less than a particular number or amount

ex.en: Children under five go free.

underline.en: draw a line under a word or piece of writing

ex.en: Underline the important words.

underline.en: a line drawn under a word or piece of writing

ex.en: There is an underline under the title.

until.en: up to the point in time when something happens

ex.en: Wait here until I come back.

up.en: toward a higher place or position

ex.en: She looked up at the sky.
Are you up yet?

us.en: used to refer to the speaker and other people as the object of a verb

ex.en: She invited us to her party.

use.en: do something with an object or method for a purpose

ex.en: Can I use your phone?
This medicine is for external use only.
I usually wake up at seven.

vacation.en: a period of time when you don't have to work or study

ex.en: We're going to the beach for vacation.

vase.en: a container used to hold flowers

ex.en: She put the roses in a vase.

vegetable.en: a plant or part of a plant that is eaten as food

ex.en: Eat more vegetables every day.

very.en: used to emphasize that you mean exactly this thing and no other

ex.en: This is the very book I was looking for.
ex.ko: 이게 바로 내가 찾던 그 책이야.

ex.en: She is very kind.

video.en: a recording of moving images, often with sound

ex.en: He posted a funny video online.

visit.en: go to see a person or place

ex.en: We visited our grandparents last weekend.

visit.en: an occasion of going to see a person or place

ex.en: Thank you for your visit.

volleyball.en: a sport in which two teams hit a ball over a net

ex.en: They play volleyball on the beach.

vote.en: choose someone or something by marking a ballot or raising your hand

ex.en: Did you vote in the election?

vote.en: an act of choosing in an election

ex.en: The vote will take place tomorrow.
There was a long wait at the airport.

wait.en: stay in one place until someone comes or something happens

ex.en: Please wait here for a moment.

waiter.en: a man who serves food and drinks in a restaurant

ex.en: The waiter brought our menus.

waitress.en: a woman who serves food and drinks in a restaurant

ex.en: The waitress took our order.
I woke up early this morning.
ex.ko: 나는 오늘 아침 일찍 잠에서 깼다.

ex.en: Please wake me up at seven.

walk.en: move by putting one foot in front of the other

ex.en: We walked to school together.

walk.en: a trip made by walking, usually for pleasure or exercise

ex.en: Let's go for a walk in the park.

wall.en: a vertical structure that separates or encloses a space

ex.en: She hung a picture on the wall.

want.en: the state of not having enough of something

ex.en: The village suffered from a want of clean water.
ex.ko: 그 마을은 깨끗한 물의 부족으로 고통받았다.

want.en: feel a desire to have or do something

ex.en: I want a cup of coffee.

war.en: armed fighting between countries or groups

ex.en: The country has suffered many years of war.

warm.en: become warm, or make something warm

ex.en: Let's warm up the soup.

warm.en: having a comfortable temperature; not cold

ex.en: It's warm outside today.
She has a warm personality.

was.en: the past tense of 'is' or 'am', used with I, he, she, or it

ex.en: She was tired yesterday.

wash.en: clean something with water and soap

ex.en: He washed his hands before eating.

wash.en: clothes that need to be washed or have just been washed

ex.en: I need to do the wash today.

water.en: pour water on plants or the ground

ex.en: She waters the plants every morning.
ex.ko: 그녀는 매일 아침 식물에 물을 준다.

water.en: the clear liquid that falls as rain and that we drink

ex.en: Can I have a glass of water?

way.en: a route or direction to get somewhere

ex.en: Can you show me the way to the station?
ex.ko: 역으로 가는 길을 알려줄 수 있어요?

way.en: a manner or method of doing something

ex.en: This is the best way to learn English.
ex.ko: 이것이 영어를 배우는 가장 좋은 방법이다.

we.en: used by a speaker to refer to himself/herself and others

ex.en: We are going to the park.

wear.en: have clothes, shoes, or accessories on your body

ex.en: She was wearing a red dress.

wear.en: become damaged or thin from continued use

ex.en: The carpet has worn thin in places.

weather.en: the condition of the sky and air at a certain time and place

ex.en: The weather is nice today.

wednesday.en: the day of the week after Tuesday and before Thursday

ex.en: We have English class on Wednesday.
I go swimming twice a week.
ex.ko: 나는 일주일에 두 번 수영하러 간다.

weekend.en: Saturday and Sunday, the days when many people don't work

ex.en: What are you doing this weekend?

welcome.en: greet someone in a friendly way when they arrive

ex.en: They welcomed us with a big smile.
You're welcome to join us.
They gave us a warm welcome.

well.en: in a good or satisfactory way

ex.en: She sings very well.
I don't feel well today.

were.en: the past tense of 'are', used with you, we, or they

ex.en: They were at home last night.

what.en: used to ask for information about something

ex.en: What is your name?

wheel.en: a round object that turns to allow a vehicle to move

ex.en: The car has four wheels.
ex.ko: 그 차는 바퀴가 네 개 있다.

wheel.en: the round device a driver holds to steer a vehicle

ex.en: He gripped the wheel tightly.

when.en: used to ask about the time something happens

ex.en: When does the movie start?

where.en: used to ask about a place or location

ex.en: Where do you live?

which.en: used to ask someone to choose or identify one of a group

ex.en: Which color do you like?

white.en: having the color of snow or milk

ex.en: She wore a white dress.

white.en: the color of snow or milk

ex.en: White is her favorite color.

who.en: used to ask which person or people

ex.en: Who is that man?

whose.en: used to ask who something belongs to

ex.en: Whose bag is this?

why.en: used to ask the reason for something

ex.en: Why are you late?

wife.en: the woman a man is married to

ex.en: His wife works as a doctor.

will.en: the mental power to decide and control your actions

ex.en: She has a strong will to succeed.

will.en: used before a verb to talk about the future

ex.en: It will rain tomorrow.

will.en: a legal document that says what happens to someone's property after they die

ex.en: He left everything to his children in his will.
ex.ko: 그는 유언장에서 모든 것을 자녀들에게 남겼다.

win.en: defeat others in a game, competition, or contest

ex.en: Our team won the match.
That was an easy win.

wind.en: follow a path that curves and turns rather than going straight

ex.en: The road winds through the mountains.

wind.en: air that is moving, sometimes strongly

ex.en: A cold wind blew through the trees.

wind.en: turn something repeatedly to tighten it, such as a clock spring

ex.en: He wound the old clock every morning.
ex.ko: 그는 매일 아침 낡은 시계태엽을 감았다.

window.en: an opening in a wall, usually covered with glass, that lets in light

ex.en: She opened the window for fresh air.

winter.cue: 일 년 중 가장 추운 계절
winter.en: the coldest season of the year

ex.en: It often snows in winter.

wish.en: want something to happen or hope for something

ex.en: I wish you good luck.
Make a wish before you blow out the candles.

with.en: in the company of someone or something

ex.en: I live with my parents.
The woman smiled at the baby.
ex.ko: 그 여자는 아기를 보고 미소 지었다.

ex.en: We had a wonderful time at the party.

word.en: a single unit of language that has meaning

ex.en: Do you know the meaning of this word?
We haven't had any word from him yet.
ex.ko: 우리는 아직 그에게서 아무 소식도 못 들었다.

work.en: do a job, especially to earn money

ex.en: She works at a hospital.

work.en: the job or activity someone does, especially for money

ex.en: I have a lot of work to do today.
My phone isn't working.

worker.en: a person who works, especially with their hands or in a particular job

ex.en: Many workers lost their jobs.

world.cue: 지구 전체와 그 안의 모든 사람
world.en: the earth and all the people and places on it

ex.en: She wants to travel around the world.
ex.ko: 그녀는 세계 곳곳을 여행하고 싶어 한다.

worry.cue: 안 좋은 일이 생길까 봐 마음 졸여
worry.en: feel anxious about something that might happen

ex.en: Don't worry, everything will be fine.

worry.en: a feeling of anxiety, or something that causes it

ex.en: Money is a big worry for them.

would.en: used to talk about hypothetical situations or make polite requests

ex.en: Would you like some coffee?

write.en: put words on paper or a screen using a pen or keyboard

ex.en: She wrote a letter to her friend.

writer.en: a person who writes books, stories, or articles

ex.en: He became a famous writer.
That's the wrong answer.
You spelled my name wrong.
ex.ko: 너 내 이름 철자를 잘못 썼어.

ex.en: It's wrong to lie to your friends.

yard.en: an outdoor area next to a house, often with grass

ex.en: The kids are playing in the yard.
I've lived here for three years.

yellow.en: having the color of a lemon or banana

ex.en: She wore a yellow shirt.

yellow.en: the color of a lemon or banana

ex.en: Yellow is a bright, cheerful color.

yes.en: used to give a positive answer or show agreement

ex.en: Yes, I agree with you.
I met her yesterday.
It's raining, yet she went for a walk.

yet.en: used in negative sentences to say something has not happened up to now

ex.en: I haven't finished my homework yet.

yogurt.en: a thick, sour food made from fermented milk

ex.en: She eats yogurt for breakfast.

you.en: used to refer to the person or people being spoken to

ex.en: You look great today.

young.en: not old; in an early stage of life

ex.en: They have three young children.

your.en: belonging to the person or people being spoken to

ex.en: Is this your bag?

yours.en: the thing or things belonging to the person being spoken to

ex.en: This pen is yours.

yourself.en: used to refer back to the person being spoken to

ex.en: Take care of yourself.

zoo.en: a place where wild animals are kept for people to see

ex.en: We took the kids to the zoo.
